design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels using scimitar melee attacks and apple projectiles while managing finite health (represented by Genie lamp smoke trail) and collecting resources to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited apples) balanced against abundant availability; health attrition from enemies and environmental hazards; checkpoint-based progression creating consequences for failure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Aladdin from the 1992 Disney film, traversing iconic settings and scenarios with expressive animation fidelity matching the source material
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Disney's first direct video game involvement with commitment to artistic authenticity: digitized hand-drawn animation from Disney animators (1000+ frames) prioritized visual fidelity over gameplay complexity. Compression of six-month schedule forced focused design. Deliberate difficulty scaling via menu settings accommodates diverse skill levels. Checkpoint system emphasizes progression accessibility while maintaining consequences. Luck-based bonus minigames and Abu token bonus levels add unpredictability and collection incentives.

difficulty.lives_system: Start with difficulty-dependent life count; extra lives earned via collecting Aladdin head icons hidden in levels or through Genie's Bonus Machine; game ends when last life lost
difficulty.continues_system: Wishes collectible via gem trading with Peddler allow continue from current level after losing last life instead of restart from beginning
difficulty.checkpointing: Blue vases act as checkpoints within levels; Aladdin revives from last checkpoint passed when losing a life
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: difficulty setting adjustable in main menu directly impacts starting life count and apple allocation; players can modulate challenge by managing difficulty selection

progression.upgrade_system: Resource collection-based: apples (finite but abundant, refillable), gems (traded for lives and wishes), Genie Tokens (unlock bonus minigame rounds), Abu Tokens (unlock bonus monkey levels), Jafar lamps (smart bombs clearing all on-screen enemies)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression through film-based levels; bonus content (Genie's Bonus Machine minigame, Abu bonus levels) unlocked by collecting specific tokens within regular levels
replayability_hooks: Hidden Aladdin head icons for extra lives encourage exploration
Luck-based Genie's Bonus Machine provides variable rewards per playthrough
Abu bonus levels offer alternative gameplay when tokens collected
Difficulty setting variation changes starting resources and challenge
Gem collection affects continue/wish availability, enabling different progression paths

world_building_techniques: Faithful adaptation of 1992 film settings and scenarios as level environments
Character presence (Genie lamp health indicator, Peddler NPC for gem trading, Jafar as enemy/lamp smart bomb source, Abu as bonus level protagonist, Iago references in hazards)
Environmental hazards thematically tied to film locations (palace guards, rocks, fish, pots)
Narrative progression follows film structure and key locations

faction_indicators: Genie (ally, represented via lamp health system and bonus mechanics)
Jafar (antagonist, represented via lamp smart bombs and minigame failure condition)
danger_signals: Health depletion (smoke trail shortening from Genie lamp)
Life loss triggering checkpoint respawn or game over
Jafar symbol on Genie's Bonus Machine ending minigame
Environmental hazards (guards, rocks, fish, pots) signaling level dangers
environmental_storytelling: Level design based on film locations establishes narrative geography
Checkpoint vases as diegetic safe zones
Peddler NPCs in each level as trading hubs maintaining world consistency
Collectible gems and tokens embedded in environments guide player exploration
Jafar lamp smart bombs as environmental resources foreshadowing antagonist presence
